Off Calendar May-01-2023 Continued To May-24-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nathan Borris appeared by zoom for Ana Robledo (administrator).
Leighton Burrey (guardian ad litem) appeared by zoom for Carter Justin Winfrey.
Robert Patterson appeared by zoom for Anna Robledo (creditor) and for Larry Rodriguez (creditor) and in the Civil case for the wrongful death of Kayla Rodriguez.

Mr. Burrey stated that he filed an opposition to the settlement agreement, that he has not had the chance to review whether there has been any discovery, that there is an irreconcilable conflict of interest between Mrs. Robledo as the administrator of the estate and as a creditor, and that he can't approve the settlement at this point. The court stated that the procedures of Probate Code 9252 was not followed, that it does not think the petition is in a position to be granted at this point and that its intent is to deny the petition without prejudice and instruct the parties to file a petition under 9252. Mr. Borris stated that the administrator timely filed a creditor's claim in this matter, that no one else was stepping forward to petition or open probate, that Anna had to file to preserve her creditor's claim and that they have an estate with a corpus of $165,000.00. Further discussion was held regarding fees that will be requested and the civil matter. The Petition for Approval of the Settlement Agreement is continued to May 24, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of a 9252 petition is also set for May 24, 2023. Mr. Boris' petition is to be scheduled for May 24, 2023.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

Garry Montinari (Insurance Defense Counsel for the representative of the Estate of Justin Winfrey) appeared by telephone.
James DuCharme appeared by zoom for Norma Banks (Trustee of the Winfrey Family Trust).
Leighton Burrey (Guardian Ad Litem for Carter Justin Lewis) appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.
Triena Trahan Bilal (Carter's Grandmother/Justin's Mother) appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

Mr. Patterson stated that hereafter he will be representing Ana as a creditor only and that Mr. Rueppel will be representing Ana as the Administrator. Mr. Burrey stated that he has been appointed as the Guardian Ad Litem and that he wants more time to review the second amended petition for letters. Ms. Greer and Mr. DuCharme raised the issue of the hearing date on the trust matter and a potential conflict. The Court stated that it is not addressing the trust at this time as it is not before it. Mr. Rueppel asked the Court to extend the special letters and stated that he has filed an ex parte petition to expand the Special Administrator's powers. Ms. Bilal informed the Court that the Grandfather's name is incorrect. Mr. DuCharme raised the issue of whether Mr. Rueppel is filing in Solano County. The Second Amended Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to May 4, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The special letters of administration are extended to May 4, 2021. Mr. Rueppel will provide a proposed order extending the letters.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Letters Of Administration
Off Calendar Jan-19-2021 Continued To Mar-09-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Greer Smith appeared by zoom for and with Jasmine Jones-Herring by zoom.
Garry Montinari (insurance defense Counsel for the representative of the Estate of Justin Winfrey) appeared by telephone.
James DuCharme appeared by zoom for Norma Banks.
Robert Patterson appeared by zoom for Ana Robledo.
John Rueppel appeared in court for Ana Robledo.

Mr. Rueppel asked the Court to extend the special letters to the hearing date on the general petition, stated that they are requesting specific extended authorities, that Ms. Jones-Herring filed a petition as a creditor which requires filing of a response and that another proceeding was filed in Solano County which seems to involve the Grandparents trust. Mr. Rueppel also stated that they are requesting to add authority to file a response as the Special Administrator and authority to accept service of the civil complaint. Ms. Greer stated that is was her understanding that the petition for special letters was filed to deal with the creditor's claim, that her Client has the right to know what additional powers are being requested and that if this is notice today, she objects because there has been no chance to respond. Mr. Patterson stated that the order embodies the fact that there is going to be a civil action. Mr. DuCharme stated that they are trying to figure out whether Ms. Greer or Mr. Patterson are going to get the civil complaint, that there is the issue of what happened to the property at time of death and whether the property remains in the trust. The Court stated that it agrees that there si a notice issue. Mr. Patterson stated that the time for Ms. Robledo to file a complaint, or lose the right to do so runs in February. Mr. DuCharme stated that he hasn't received anything on the petition for general letters. Mr. Rueppel stated that he is happy to serve a copy on Mr. DuCharme. The Second Amended Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to March 9, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The special letters of administration are extended to March 9, 2021.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
